title: Track collection activity

publish.description: Read the Activity tab — CTR before and now, the uplift it produced, and the run history log.

intro: Once a collection is sorting, the Activity tab tells you whether it's working. It compares click-through before and after qSortBy took over, shows the uplift, and keeps a log of every sort run.

steps.heading: Open the Activity tab
steps.body: Get there from **Left menu → Collections → open a collection → the Activity tab**. This is the collection's report card — proof of what the sorting has done, in one view.

els.text: Left menu → Collections → open a collection → the Activity tab — its report card for how sorting is performing.

steps.heading: Compare CTR and read the uplift
steps.body: The top of the tab shows **CTR before** qSortBy sorted the collection next to **CTR now**, with the **uplift** between them. A positive uplift means the sort rule is putting the products shoppers click on higher up.

els.text: CTR before vs now, with the uplift — positive means sorting is helping.

notes.text: Give a new sort rule a few days of traffic before judging the uplift — early numbers move around.

steps.heading: Review the run history
steps.body: Below the metrics, the **run history** table logs every sort — when it ran, what triggered it, and how the order changed. Use it to confirm the collection is re-sorting on the cadence you set and to trace any surprising change back to a specific run.

els.text: The run history logs every sort — when it ran and how the order changed.

outro: The Activity tab closes the loop: it shows the CTR before and after, the uplift qSortBy produced, and a full run history so you can trust that sorting is doing its job.
